questions.question: What is a quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral has four sides, four vertices, and four angles.

questions.question: What is a rectangle?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral in which all angles are 90°
questions.explanation: In a rectangle, opposite sides are equal and parallel.

questions.question: What is a square?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral with all sides equal and all angles 90°
questions.explanation: A square is a special type of rectangle.

questions.question: What is the sum of the angles of a quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral can be divided into two triangles, each having 180°.

questions.question: What is a parallelogram?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral in which opposite sides are parallel
questions.explanation: Opposite sides are also equal in length.

questions.question: What is a property of opposite angles in a parallelogram?
What happens to the diagonals of a rectangle?
questions.correct_answer: They are equal and bisect each other
questions.explanation: Both diagonals meet at their midpoint.

questions.question: What happens to the diagonals of a square?
questions.correct_answer: They are equal, bisect each other, and intersect at right angles
questions.explanation: They also bisect the angles of the square.

questions.question: What is the relationship between rectangles and parallelograms?
questions.explanation: A rectangle satisfies the definition of a parallelogram.

questions.question: Why is every square also a rectangle?
questions.correct_answer: Because all angles of a square are 90°
questions.explanation: A rectangle only requires four right angles.

notes.title: Quadrilateral
notes.content: A quadrilateral is a polygon with four sides, four vertices, and four angles.

notes.title: Rectangle Properties
notes.content: Opposite sides are equal and parallel, all angles are 90°, and diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

notes.title: Square Properties
notes.content: All sides are equal, all angles are 90°, diagonals are equal and intersect at right angles.

notes.title: Parallelogram Properties
notes.content: Opposite sides are parallel and equal, opposite angles are equal, and diagonals bisect each other.

notes.title: Angle Sum Property
notes.content: The sum of interior angles of any quadrilateral is always 360°.

facts.fact: A quadrilateral can be divided into two triangles.
Every square is a rectangle and also a parallelogram.
Opposite angles of a parallelogram are always equal.
The diagonals of a square bisect its angles.
